title: sassea urticae
intro: Sassea urticae, also known as the Stinging Nettle Tree, is a plant species that belongs to the family Boraginaceae. It is characterized by its stinging hairs and is often used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant's leaves and stems have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as arthritis, eczema, and skin irritations, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Sassea urticae can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are commonly used to alleviate symptoms of allergies, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
